reason: Both answers correctly identify the 100% exclusion under §1202 (post-9/27/2010 acquisition), note the $8M gain fits under the $10M cap, and correctly state no AMT preference and no NIIT on the excluded gain. Both appropriately caveat that this depends on the corporation meeting QSBS requirements at issuance and suggest verification. Answer A adds a relevant and practically important point that Answer B omits: state tax may still apply, specifically calling out that California doesn't conform to QSBS treatment and would tax the full gain — this is a materially useful caveat for a "roughly what tax will I owe" question, since state tax could be a large additional liability the asker needs to plan for. This makes A slightly more complete without adding unnecessary padding.
